faqs.question: What pantry pests are most common in Union, NJ homes?
faqs.answer: The most common pantry pests in Union include Indian meal moths, cigarette beetles, saw-toothed grain beetles, and flour beetles. These pests typically arrive in contaminated food products from stores and quickly infest cereals, grains, dried fruits, nuts, and spices in your pantry.

faqs.question: How much does pantry pest control cost in Union?
faqs.answer: Our pantry pest control service in Union ranges from $350 to $550, depending on the severity of the infestation and the size of your home. This flat rate includes a complete inspection, treatment of all affected areas, disposal recommendations, and prevention advice to keep pests from returning.

faqs.question: How quickly can you respond to pantry pest problems in Union?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day service throughout Union, NJ for pantry pest emergencies. Most appointments can be scheduled within 24 hours of your call. We understand how distressing it is to discover pests in your food storage areas and prioritize rapid response for all Union residents.

faqs.question: Do I need to throw away all my pantry food after treatment?
faqs.answer: Not necessarily all of it. During our inspection, we'll identify which items are infested and must be discarded. Sealed, unopened containers are often safe to keep. After treatment, we'll provide specific guidance on what to discard and how to properly store remaining food items to prevent future infestations in your Union home.
